## Taiwan Semiconductor Estimated at Roughly 9% Above Fair Value on Q2 Optimism

If TSM carries a modest cyclical premium, a Q2 beat-and-raise becomes the base case to hold the multiple; any disappointment on advanced node utilization or forward guidance could unwind that premium quickly.

- A third-party valuation analysis suggests TSM may be approximately 9% above estimated fair value.
- The premium is attributed to Q2 demand optimism rather than a structural re-rating.
- This is a valuation opinion piece, not a fundamental data release from the company.
- Foundry capacity and semiconductor cycle themes underpin the optimism being priced.
